definitions.text: A competitive sporting event such as a boxing meet (commonly called a "bout"), a baseball game, or a cricket match.
examples: My local team are playing in a match against their arch-rivals today.

definitions.text: Any contest or trial of strength or skill, or to determine superiority.
To agree; to be equal; to correspond.
examples: Their interests didn't match, so it took a long time to agree what to do together.
These two copies are supposed to be identical, but they don't match.

definitions.text: To agree with; to be equal to; to correspond to.
examples: His interests didn't match her interests.

definitions.text: To equal or exceed in achievement.
examples: She matched him at every turn: anything he could do, she could do as well or better.

definitions.text: A small wooden stick specially treated with chemicals to produce flame upon contact with another chemical, from the frictional heat produced by striking it against a surface, or both; other similar items made from chemically-treated paper, cardboard, etc.
examples: He struck a match and lit his pipe.

definitions.text: Synonym of taper, a thin stick used to transfer flame, as from tender to a candle.
